title: Story 1.3: Three-Layer RBAC Framework
description:
Implement role-based permission enforcement at router (GoRouter guard), Riverpod provider (data visibility), and domain operation (PermissionDeniedException) layers using a single shared predicate function hasPermission(Role, Permission).

Define Role enum: doctor, assistant, nurse.

Define Permission enum with action-based entries. The 5 Doctor-only permissions are:
1. canManageClinic — edit clinic profile
2. canManageStaff — add/remove assistants and nurses
3. canAssignRoles — assign roles and permissions to users
4. canViewFinances — view full financial data
5. canUnlockVisit — unlock a completed visit

Unit test enumerates these 5 and confirms doctor=true, assistant=false, nurse=false for each. Also confirms all three layers (router guard, Riverpod provider, domain requirePermission) delegate to the same hasPermission predicate. Part of Epic 1 (DocCentral-jkt).
close_reason: Closed

title: Story 11.4: Conflict Resolution & User Notification
description: Last-write-wins conflict resolution based on UTC updated_at. Non-financial records: remote wins if newer, local wins if newer, discarded side logged. Invoice and Payment conflicts: surfaced as visible user warning (not silent), last-write-wins applied as default but warning persists until dismissed. No data permanently lost; losing version logged for forensic use. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 11.3: Pull Remote Changes from Supabase
description: When online, sync engine pulls remote changes incrementally (since last successful pull timestamp) and applies to local Drift DB. Pull failures mid-way leave local data valid and consistent; pull resumes from last safe checkpoint. After successful pull, updated data visible on affected screens without manual refresh. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 11.2: Push Local Changes to Supabase
description: On regaining connectivity, sync engine pushes all pending records (sync_status=pending) to Supabase in batches. After successful push, records transition to synced. Push failures retry with exponential backoff capped at 10 minutes. Push is non-blocking: UI remains fully responsive during sync. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 11.1: Full Offline Functionality Verification
description: Verify all DocCentral workflows complete without internet. End-to-end test with network disabled: UJ-2 (receive patient + open Patient File), UJ-3 (wrap up visit + take payment), UJ-4 (handle cancellation), UJ-10 (day closeout) all complete successfully. No screen is gated by connectivity except initial sign-in on fresh device. Records created offline have sync_status=pending. Part of Epic 11 (DocCentral-gys).

title: Story 7.5: View Outstanding Balance & Patients with Balance
description: Patient File shows Outstanding Balance = sum(Invoice.total - sum(Payments)) across non-void Invoices, computed at read time, updates within 100ms of a Payment. Dedicated screen lists patients with Outstanding Balance > 0, sortable by balance descending and by days-since-last-payment. Tapping a row navigates to that patient's Patient File. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).
notes:
COMPLETE. Implemented via a Drift join query (leftOuterJoin/innerJoin, no raw SQL) rather than customSelect, for lower API-version risk and automatic reactivity to all joined tables:
- InvoiceRepositoryImpl.watchOutstandingBalanceForPatient(role, patientId): joins Invoices+Payments for the patient (excluding voided/soft-deleted invoices), sums (total-paid) per invoice in Dart, returns the grand total. Gated by canEditInvoice (Doctor+Assistant, matches how InvoiceDetailPage's view-gate works).
- InvoiceRepositoryImpl.watchPatientsWithBalance(role): joins Patients+Invoices+Payments, groups per-patient in Dart, filters balance>0, tracks each patient's most recent payment date (null if none). Gated by canViewFinances (Doctor-only) matching that permission's apparent 'financial report' intent.
New PatientBalance domain model (patient: PatientRecord, balance, lastPaymentDate?).

Patient File (patient_detail_pane.dart) now shows the real computed balance instead of the Epic-7-not-built placeholder zero. New PatientsWithBalancePage (route /patients/balance, Doctor-only via permissionRouteGuard) lists patients with balance>0, sortable via a dropdown (Balance descending / Days since last payment descending, treating never-paid as an extreme large value so those patients sort first), tapping a row calls selectedPatientProvider.select() + goNamed(patients).

IMPORTANT bug found+fixed: selectedPatientProvider was plain autoDispose. This was previously safe by accident -- CalendarPage/_viewPatientFile's navigation to Patients happens within the app shell's StatefulShellRoute.indexedStack, which never fully unmounts branches, so the provider was never actually disposed in practice. But PatientsWithBalancePage is a plain top-level GoRoute OUTSIDE the shell -- navigating away from it fully disposes it, and since nothing else was watching the provider at that instant, autoDispose wiped the selection before the destination page (PatientListPage, which does watch it) could read it. Confirmed via a real widget test failure, not a hunch. Fixed by marking @Riverpod(keepAlive: true). This is the same bug class documented in memory for appRouterProvider/currentRoleProvider/AppointmentController, now a 4th confirmed instance, and the first one caused by 'route topology' (inside vs outside the shell) rather than a same-call-twice pattern.

Also fixed a RenderFlex overflow: the Patients page AppBar's 'Add patient' (FilledButton.icon) plus new 'Patients with balance' button together didn't fit at the router test's ~583px viewport (narrower than a bare page test due to the NavigationRail chrome). Converted the newer, secondary action to an icon-only IconButton+tooltip rather than restructuring into a Wrap, since only one of the two buttons needed to shrink.

Tests: 10 new repository tests (balance computation across multiple invoices/payments recomputed after each payment, excludes voided, permission checks; patients-with-balance list filtering/grouping/lastPaymentDate/soft-delete-exclusion/permission), 5 new PatientsWithBalancePage widget tests (empty state, list rendering, no-payment-yet state, default sort order, tap-to-navigate -- this last test is what caught the keepAlive bug). Full suite: flutter analyze clean, 262/262 tests passing. Committed locally as 8455595 (not pushed, per standing instruction).
close_reason: Story 7.5 complete: outstanding balance computation, Patients with Balance screen, real Patient File balance display, and full test coverage all implemented and passing.

title: Story 7.4: Void Invoice
description: Dentist voids any non-void Invoice with a required reason: transitions to void, records reason + UTC timestamp, Invoice contributes 0 to Outstanding Balance. Prior Payments preserved and shown as refund owed. Void Invoice remains visible in financial history. Assistant returns PERMISSION_DENIED. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).
notes:
COMPLETE. Added new Doctor-only Permission.canVoidInvoice (Assistant/Nurse get PermissionDeniedException). Implemented InvoiceRepositoryImpl.voidInvoice(role, actorUserId, invoiceId, reason): rejects an already-voided invoice (InvoiceAlreadyVoidedException) but otherwise allows voiding from ANY status (draft/unpaid/partially_paid/paid) per the AC's 'any non-void Invoice' wording -- unlike unlockVisit/finalizeInvoice which are draft-gated, voidInvoice has no such precondition. Transitions status->voided and logs actor+reason+UTC timestamp (via createdAt) to a new InvoiceVoids table, schema v15->v16. Prior Invoice Items and Payments are left completely untouched (no deletion/mutation) satisfying 'Prior Payments preserved'.

InvoiceDetailPage: added a 'Void invoice' action (Doctor-only via canVoidInvoice, hidden once already voided) with a required-reason confirmation dialog (_VoidInvoiceDialog, mirrors the existing _UnlockVisitDialog pattern from Story 5.5). Added a 'Refund owed' label shown only when status==voided AND sum(payments)>0, displaying the total prior payments as the refund amount owed -- satisfies 'contributes 0 to Outstanding Balance... Payments preserved and shown as refund owed'. 'Void invoice remains visible in financial history' is satisfied by construction: nothing filters voided invoices out of InvoiceDetailPage or its providers.

Also updated test/shared/domain/rbac/permission_checker_test.dart's hardcoded doctorOnly permission list (6->7) to include the new canVoidInvoice permission.

Tests: 4 new repository tests (voids from any status incl. paid + logs actor/reason, preserves prior Items unchanged, rejects double-void, rejects Assistant), schema v16 test, 8 new widget tests (button visibility for Assistant/already-voided, cancel dialog, blank-reason validation, successful void+snackbar+button-disappears, already-voided error snackbar, refund-owed label shown/hidden based on prior payments). Full suite: flutter analyze clean, 250/250 tests passing. Committed locally as 72823b3 (not pushed, per standing instruction).
close_reason: Story 7.4 complete: voidInvoice repository method, new canVoidInvoice permission, InvoiceDetailPage void/refund-owed UI, and full test coverage all implemented and passing.

title: Story 7.3: Record Payment (Full & Partial)
description: Assistant records a Payment on a non-void Invoice: amount, method (default cash), date (default today), optional notes. Saved with recorded_by_user_id and UTC timestamp. Invoice status derived: partially_paid if sum(Payments) < total, paid if >= total. Payment on draft Invoice auto-finalizes it. Multiple Payments per Invoice supported. Payments are immutable (no delete/edit). Unit test confirms Invoice status is always derived. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).
notes:
COMPLETE. Implemented PaymentRepositoryImpl.recordPayment(role, actorUserId, invoiceId, amount, method=cash, paymentDate=today-if-omitted, notes): requires canRecordPayment, rejects non-positive amount (PaymentValidationException), rejects voided invoice (PaymentInvoiceVoidedException). If invoice is still draft, auto-finalizes it first (same draft->unpaid transition + InvoiceFinalizations log as Story 7.2's finalizeInvoice, inlined rather than cross-repository call). Inserts an immutable Payment row (no update/delete method exists on the repository interface at all), then recomputes invoice.status from sum(all payments) vs total -- partially_paid while under, paid once >= total -- never a directly-writable field. New Payments table (@DataClassName('PaymentRow') to avoid colliding with the domain Payment model, mirroring the established Roles/PerformedTreatments/InvoiceItems pattern), schemaVersion 14->15.

New providers: payment_repository_provider, payments_for_invoice_provider, payment_controller_provider. InvoiceDetailPage: added an invoice status label, a Payments section (fixed-height scrollable list, read-only rows with method+date+notes+amount, no edit/remove UI since payments are immutable), and a 'Record payment' button (hidden once voided) opening a dialog (amount, method dropdown defaulting to cash, date picker defaulting to today, optional notes).

Fixed a RenderFlex overflow: with 3 possible action buttons (Add adjustment/Finalize invoice/Record payment) all able to appear together on a draft invoice, the AppBar.actions Row no longer fit at 800px test width. Moved all three into a Wrap in the body below the total/status line (same fix pattern as the earlier _AppointmentRow overflow from Epic 5).

Tests: 10 repository tests (partial->partially_paid with explicit 'status always derived, never directly writable' verification, full->paid, multiple payments accumulate, overpayment->paid, draft auto-finalizes first, saves recorded_by_user_id/method/date/notes, defaults to cash/today when omitted, voided rejection, non-positive-amount rejection, Nurse permission-denied), schema v15 test, 8 new widget tests (status+empty-payments display, shows recorded payments, button visibility for Nurse/voided, successful submission+snackbar, non-positive-amount client validation, voided-invoice error snackbar). Full suite: flutter analyze clean, 237/237 tests passing. Committed locally as 710d305 (not pushed, per standing instruction).
close_reason: Story 7.3 complete: recordPayment repository method, derived invoice status, InvoiceDetailPage payments UI, and full test coverage all implemented and passing.

title: Story 7.1: Review & Adjust Draft Invoice
description: After Visit completion, Assistant opens the linked Invoice in draft status. Each Invoice Item mirrors a Performed Treatment (description, tooth number, quantity, unit price, total_price). Assistant adds discount or surcharge adjustments as explicit Invoice Items with adjustment_type. Invoice total = sum(treatment items) + sum(adjustments). Adjustments rejected once Invoice exits draft. Unit test confirms total is never a directly writable field. Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).
notes: COMPLETE. Implemented InvoiceRepositoryImpl.addAdjustment(role, invoiceId, adjustmentType, description, amount): requires canEditInvoice, rejects amount<=0 (InvoiceAdjustmentValidationException), rejects non-draft invoice (InvoiceNotDraftException). Inserts an InvoiceItem with adjustmentType set (discount -> totalPrice=-amount, surcharge -> totalPrice=+amount), then recomputes Invoices.totalAmount as sum of ALL item totalPrice values in the same transaction -- total is never a directly-writable parameter anywhere in the repository, satisfying the AC's explicit requirement. Fixed a class-name collision from Story 5.4: added @DataClassName('InvoiceItemRow') to the InvoiceItems table since the new domain model is named InvoiceItem (same pattern as Roles->RoleRow, PerformedTreatments->PerformedTreatmentRow). Updated Story 5.4's visit_repository_impl_test.dart to reference InvoiceItemRow for the Drift row type. New InvoiceDetailPage (providers: invoice_repository/invoice_for_visit/invoice_items/invoice_controller) reached via a 'View invoice' button on VisitDetailPage (visible when canEditInvoice && visit completed) -- shows the total + item list, with an 'Add adjustment' action (discount/surcharge dropdown + description + amount) gated to draft invoices only. Tests: 8 repository tests (discount reduces total, surcharge increases total, multiple adjustments stack with recomputed running total, the explicit 'total is never directly writable' verification test, not-draft rejection, zero/negative amount rejection, Nurse permission-denied), 7 widget tests (shows total+items, empty state, Add-adjustment visibility gated by role and draft status, successful submission updates list+total, error snackbar for non-draft, client-side validation for non-positive amount). Full suite: flutter analyze clean, 209/209 tests passing. Committed locally as 55c135a (not pushed, per standing instruction).
close_reason: Story 7.1 complete: addAdjustment repository method, InvoiceDetailPage UI, and full test coverage all implemented and passing.

title: Story 7.2: Finalize Invoice (No-Payment Path)
description: Assistant finalizes a draft Invoice without payment: transitions draft to unpaid, locks Invoice Items and adjustments, logs actor + UTC timestamp. Dentist can unlock parent Visit (if no Payments) to revert Invoice to draft. If Invoice has a Payment, unlock rejected (must void first). Part of Epic 7 (DocCentral-0y8).
notes:
COMPLETE. Implemented InvoiceRepositoryImpl.finalizeInvoice(role, actorUserId, invoiceId): requires canEditInvoice, rejects non-draft invoice (InvoiceNotDraftException). Transitions Invoice.status draft->unpaid and logs actor+UTC timestamp to a new InvoiceFinalizations table (schema v13->v14, createdAt from SyncMetadata serves as the timestamp). Items/adjustments are locked implicitly since addAdjustment (Story 7.1) already rejects any non-draft invoice.

IMPORTANT scope reconciliation with Story 5.5: this story's AC ('Dentist can unlock parent Visit if no Payments to revert Invoice to draft') directly conflicts with 5.5's original stricter reading (which rejected ANY non-draft invoice via VisitInvoiceFinalizedException, including a merely-finalized unpaid one). Resolved by revising VisitRepositoryImpl.unlockVisit: unlock is now allowed when the linked Invoice is draft OR unpaid (finalized, no payment) -- in the unpaid case the Invoice is also reverted to draft as part of the same unlock transaction. VisitInvoiceFinalizedException is now reserved for voided invoices only (a genuine dead end). VisitInvoiceHasPaymentsException (partially_paid/paid) is unchanged. Updated visit_exceptions.dart/visit_repository.dart doc comments and the visitUnlockInvoiceFinalizedError ARB text (now voided-specific) across all 3 locales to match.

New InvoiceDetailPage 'Finalize invoice' button (draft + canEditInvoice only) with a confirmation dialog, wired through InvoiceController.finalizeInvoice.

Tests: 3 new repository tests for finalizeInvoice (transitions to unpaid + logs actor, rejects non-draft, rejects Nurse), updated visit_repository_impl_test.dart's unlockVisit group (voided-only rejection test + new success-reverts-to-draft-when-unpaid test), 6 new widget tests for the Finalize invoice button/dialog/success/error flow, updated visit_detail_page_test.dart's finalized-error test to expect the voided-specific message, schema v14 test. Full suite: flutter analyze clean, 219/219 tests passing. Committed locally as a1fd258 (not pushed, per standing instruction).
close_reason: Story 7.2 complete: finalizeInvoice repository method, revised unlockVisit semantics per this story's clarified AC, InvoiceDetailPage UI, and full test coverage all implemented and passing.

title: Story 5.4: Complete Visit & Auto-Generate Invoice
description: Assistant completes an in_progress Visit: status becomes completed, ended_at set, Performed Treatments locked, exactly one Invoice in draft created linked to the Visit with Invoice Items mirroring each Performed Treatment (description, tooth number, quantity, unit price, total_price). Completed Visit rejects all field edits. Unit test confirms one-and-only-one Invoice per completion with Items matching Performed Treatments exactly. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).
notes: Implemented VisitRepositoryImpl.completeVisit: requires VisitStatus.inProgress (else VisitNotEditableException), requires at least one non-soft-deleted PerformedTreatment (else VisitRequiresTreatmentException), then atomically (single transaction) sets Visit.status=completed + endedAt, creates one draft Invoice (totalAmount = sum of unitPrice*quantity) and one InvoiceItem per PerformedTreatment (description/toothNumber/quantity/unitPrice/totalPrice mirrored exactly). Added Invoices/InvoiceItems tables, schemaVersion bumped 11->12. VisitController.completeVisit wired (not keepAlive - single invocation per confirm dialog, no retry-across-await pattern). VisitDetailPage: Complete visit button shown only when canCompleteVisit && status==inProgress && hasTreatments; confirmation dialog; success snackbar; error handling for both exception types. Tests: 4 repository tests (exactly-one-invoice-with-matching-items is the key AC test, plus not-in-progress, no-treatments, soft-deleted-treatments-ignored), 5 widget tests (button visibility x2, confirm dialog cancel, confirm+success+snackbar+button-disappears), schema v12 test. Full suite: flutter analyze clean, 181/181 tests passing. Committed locally (not pushed, per standing instruction).
close_reason: Story 5.4 complete: completeVisit repository method, controller, UI, and full test coverage all implemented and passing.

title: Story 5.3: Record Clinical Notes & Diagnosis
description: On an in_progress Visit: edit diagnosis and clinical_notes as multi-line text, autosaved on field blur (no explicit save button). On a completed Visit: both fields are read-only. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).
notes: Added Visits.diagnosis/clinicalNotes columns (schema v10->v11) and VisitRepository.updateClinicalRecord (requires status==inProgress else VisitNotEditableException, reuses canAddClinicalNotes permission -- Doctor+Assistant+Nurse -- since that permission was clearly named for exactly this feature). UI: new _ClinicalRecordSection on VisitDetailPage, two multi-line TextFields with FocusNode listeners that save on focus-loss (blur) rather than any explicit button, satisfying the 'no explicit save button' AC. Fields switch to readOnly:true once the Visit is completed, still showing the content captured at completion time (pre-filled from the existing VisitRecord, not cleared). Tests: repository tests (save on in_progress, rejection on wrong status), schema v11 test, widget tests including one that actually drives focus-blur (tap into diagnosis, type, move focus to notes field, unfocus) and asserts the fake repository received the saved value -- verifying the autosave-without-a-button behavior end to end. Also verified read-only rendering + pre-filled content on a completed visit. flutter analyze clean, 172/172 tests pass (confirmed via a clean foreground run after an earlier run stalled due to accumulated stray background processes from earlier in the session, not a real test hang).
close_reason: Story 5.3 implemented, tested, and committed (44eeab4).

title: Story 5.2: Record Performed Treatments
description: On an in_progress Visit: add Performed Treatments (tooth number, procedure name free text, unit price, quantity), edit or remove them. Each treatment timestamped and attributed to recording user. On a completed Visit: edits and removals rejected. Unit test confirms Performed Treatments cannot be modified on a completed Visit. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).
notes: Built a real VisitDetailPage (no such screen existed before this story) as the natural home for Performed Treatments, hosting PerformedTreatmentRepository (add/update/remove, all requiring the parent Visit be in_progress else VisitNotEditableException, new @DataClassName('PerformedTreatmentRow') Drift row to avoid colliding with the domain model name like the earlier Roles/Role collision). Treatments soft-delete via the existing SyncMetadata pattern. Add/edit/remove controls are hidden in the UI whenever the Visit isn't in_progress (completed-lock enforced both server- and client-side). Wired a new 'View visit' icon into checked-in/in-progress calendar rows reusing Story 5.1's appointment->visit lookup; had to restructure _AppointmentRow's action icons from a single Row into a Wrap since it can now host up to 6 icons/badges and was overflowing on narrower widths. New performed_treatments table, schema v9->v10. Tests: repository tests (add w/ attribution+timestamp, edit, remove, and the explicit beads AC unit test confirming Performed Treatments cannot be modified on a completed Visit for both edit and remove), schema v10 test, VisitDetailPage widget tests (empty state, rendering, add flow, remove flow, controls-hidden-when-completed). flutter analyze clean, 167/167 tests pass.
close_reason: Story 5.2 implemented, tested, and committed (fbcb484).

title: Story 5.1: Start Visit & Mark In-Progress
description: Assistant transitions a checked_in Visit to in_progress. Captures in_progress_at as UTC now(). Controls for adding Performed Treatments, editing diagnosis, and editing clinical notes become enabled. Integration test confirms in_progress_at is non-null after transition. Part of Epic 5 (DocCentral-5mr).
notes: Added VisitRepository.startProgress(role, appointmentId): looks up the Visit by appointmentId (1:1 relationship established at check-in, Story 4.5), requires status==checkedIn (else VisitNotEditableException, new exception type for wrong-Visit-state operations, mirroring AppointmentNotEditableException's role), transitions to inProgress and sets inProgressAt=UTC now(). Added watchVisitForAppointment so the calendar can look up a checked-in appointment's Visit state without exposing Visit ids to the appointment feature. New visits.in_progress_at column, schema v8->v9. UI: 'Start Visit' action on checked-in appointment rows (canEditVisit-gated), replaced by an 'In progress' text indicator once started. AC's second bullet ('controls for Performed Treatments/diagnosis/notes become enabled') is NOT built here -- those controls are Story 5.2/5.3's actual scope; this story only covers the state transition + its trigger, consistent with how the epics.md breaks down the Visit lifecycle across stories. Tests: integration test explicitly required by the AC confirming in_progress_at is non-null after transition, wrong-state rejection test, watchVisitForAppointment null/found tests, schema v9 test, widget tests for the Start Visit button appearing/transitioning/disappearing. flutter analyze clean, 155/155 tests pass.
close_reason: Story 5.1 implemented, tested, and committed (28403c1).

title: Story 4.5: Check In Patient
description: Assistant taps Check In on a scheduled appointment: transitions to checked_in, creates a Visit in checked_in status linked to appointment and patient, sets Visit.started_at = now(). Patient File accessible from same screen showing last 3 visits, Treatment Plan, Outstanding Balance, and Attachments. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).
notes: Added minimal Visit feature (domain/data/presentation) scoped to exactly what this story needs: VisitRepository.checkIn (transaction: appointment scheduled->checkedIn + insert Visits row with started_at=now UTC, dentistId=appointment.assignedUserId) and watchRecentVisits(patientId, limit=3). New visits table, schema v7->v8. Diagnosis/notes/inProgressAt/endedAt intentionally NOT added yet -- Epic 5 (Story 5.1+) will extend this table via its own migration when it needs them, avoiding speculative columns now. Check-In action added to calendar rows (gated on existing canCheckInPatient permission, Doctor+Assistant); once checked_in, a new 'View Patient File' action jumps to the Patients tab with that patient pre-selected via selectedPatientProvider + context.goNamed -- this is the 'Patient File accessible from the same screen' AC. Patient File's Recent Visits section now shows real data (last 3, newest first) instead of the old 'not available yet' placeholder text (reworded patientNoVisitsYet to 'No visits yet' across all 3 locales since the feature is genuinely available now); Treatment Plan/Outstanding Balance/Attachments remain honest placeholders (Epic 6/7/9 not built). Also fixed patient_list_page_test.dart which was silently touching the real native database (missing appointmentRepositoryProvider/visitRepositoryProvider overrides) -- now fully hermetic. flutter analyze clean, 139/139 tests pass.
close_reason: Story 4.5 implemented, tested, and committed (3b99d64).

title: Story 4.4: Cancel & Reschedule Appointment
description: Assistant cancels a scheduled appointment with required reason (patient_cancelled, no_show, clinic_cancelled, rescheduled). Cancellation record created with timestamp, actor, reason. Slot freed in calendar. Rescheduled reason prompts creating replacement appointment linked via rescheduled_to_appointment_id before completing cancellation. No-show pattern flagged on Patient File for repeat offenders. Unit test confirms Cancellation record always has non-null reason/actor/timestamp. Part of Epic 4 (DocCentral-rkm).

title: Story 2.4: Language Switch & Localized Formats
description: User switches app language between FR/AR/EN from Settings. Selection takes effect immediately without restart. Arabic switches entire layout to RTL (navigation, form fields, lists, modals). Dates format per locale (FR: DD/MM/YYYY, EN: YYYY-MM-DD), currency as TND, time in 24h. Language preference persists per User across sessions. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).
notes:
Implemented and tested (4 new tests + 1 fixed, 86/86 total passing; 0 analyzer issues). Mostly wired up existing infrastructure from Story 1.1/1.4 rather than building new plumbing.

Language switch UI: added a DropdownButton<Locale> to Settings, wired to the existing appLocaleProvider (built in Story 1.4/1.1, already had setLocale + SharedPreferences persistence, never had a UI trigger before now). Shows each language's own native name (Français/English/العربية) - deliberately NOT run through the l10n system, since translating a language's own name is not standard i18n practice.

REAL BEHAVIOR CHANGE (not just wiring): LocaleFormatterService.formatDate was hardcoded to dd/MM/yyyy for every locale, which contradicted this story's explicit AC ("FR: DD/MM/YYYY, EN: YYYY-MM-DD"). Fixed to vary by locale.languageCode. Updated the pre-existing locale_formatter_service_test.dart, which had asserted the old (now-incorrect) EN behavior.

"Selection takes effect immediately without restart": already true for free, since DocCentralApp's MaterialApp.router already reactively binds locale: ref.watch(appLocaleProvider) - no extra plumbing needed.

"Arabic switches entire layout to RTL": already true for free via Flutter's standard Locale -> Directionality inference (GlobalMaterialLocalizations supports ar) - verified with a dedicated widget test asserting Directionality.of ends up TextDirection.rtl after switching to Arabic through the real Settings UI, not just checking provider state.

DESIGN DECISION: "Language preference persists per User" is satisfied via the existing device-level SharedPreferences persistence (survives restart), not a new per-Users-table-row locale column. Given this is a single-shared-device clinic tool with one signed-in user active at a time, device-level persistence functions as that user's preference in practice. Flagging this interpretation in case a true multi-user-per-device scenario becomes a real requirement later.
close_reason: Language switch UI wired to existing appLocaleProvider; fixed a real locale-formatting bug (EN date format contradicted the AC); verified RTL flip end-to-end. 86/86 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 2.3: Add Staff User (Assistant / Nurse)
description: Dentist adds a new Assistant or Nurse User from Clinic Settings with name, email, role, and initial password. New User can sign in with assigned Role permissions. Assistant cannot access Add Staff User screen (hidden + router guard blocked). Domain layer returns PERMISSION_DENIED when non-Dentist calls add-user operation. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).
notes:
Implemented and tested (13 new tests, 83/83 total passing; 0 analyzer issues). Reuses infrastructure from Stories 2.1/2.2.

ClinicRepository.addStaffUser(actingRole, firstName, lastName, email, password, role): requirePermission(actingRole, Permission.canManageStaff) first (throws PermissionDeniedException for non-Dentist, matches beads AC's "Domain layer returns PERMISSION_DENIED"), then reuses the same "auth signUp first, then atomic local transaction" pattern as provisionClinic - inserts a User (isClinicOwner: false) + links to the EXISTING Assistant/Nurse Role row (created back in Story 2.1's provisioning, not duplicated).

All three RBAC layers now demonstrably wired for this operation:
- Layer 2 (hidden): Settings page only shows the "Add staff" button when permissionCheckerProvider(canManageStaff) is true.
- Layer 1 (router guard): first-ever real use of permission_route_guard.dart's permissionRouteGuard (built in Story 1.3, sat unused until now) - wired as a per-route redirect on the new /settings/add-staff GoRoute, denying non-Dentist roles back to /settings.
- Layer 3 (domain): requirePermission as above.

Role dropdown offers Assistant/Nurse only (not Dentist - single-owner-per-clinic in this schema).

MAJOR BUG FOUND AND FIXED: currentRoleProvider (CurrentRole) was NOT keepAlive (default @riverpod = autoDispose). Since it's set imperatively via .notifier.setRole(...) from sign-up/sign-in with no persistent widget watcher guaranteed, it was silently resetting to null between being set and being read by the new addStaffUser route's permission guard - a real, reproducible bug (not just a test artifact), caught via a dedicated router-guard test that isolates the redirect without a full app tree. Fixed by marking CurrentRole @Riverpod(keepAlive: true), same fix class as appRouterProvider in Story 2.1. Saved to memory since this pattern will likely recur for any future session-lifetime provider read via .notifier rather than continuous ref.watch.
close_reason: Add Staff User implemented with all 3 RBAC layers wired (first real use of the router guard from Story 1.3); found and fixed a real currentRoleProvider keepAlive bug along the way. 83/83 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 2.2: Email + Password Sign-In with Persistent Session
description: Users sign in with email and password and land on Today Calendar. Session persists across app restarts via locally cached credentials. Reopening the app reuses the session. Sign-out clears session and returns to sign-in screen. Device offline with cached session: user can sign in and access all offline features. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).
notes:
Implemented and tested (8 new tests, 75/75 total passing; 0 analyzer issues). Built directly on Story 2.1's auth/clinic infrastructure.

Added AuthService.signIn/signOut (Supabase signInWithPassword/signOut) and ClinicRepository.resolveRole(authUserId) (queries Users -> UserRoles -> Roles locally, maps the stored role name string back to the Role enum via Role.values.asNameMap()).

New reactive session layer: currentSessionProvider (Stream<Session?> wrapping Supabase's onAuthStateChange, which emits an initial event with the current persisted session on subscription - this is what makes "reopen app, reuse session" work without extra plumbing) -> resolvedRoleProvider (Future<Role?>, resolves the local role for the active session's user, or null if signed out).

Router redirect extended (renamed _HasLocalClinicRefreshNotifier -> _AuthRefreshNotifier, now listens to both hasLocalClinicProvider and resolvedRoleProvider): no clinic -> /sign-up; clinic exists but no resolved role (not signed in) -> /sign-in; both satisfied -> shell. Order matters: sign-up check happens before sign-in check.

New /sign-in route + SignInPage + SignInController. On successful sign-in, resolves role from local DB and calls currentRoleProvider.setRole(...), then navigates to Calendar explicitly (mirrors Story 2.1's sign-up flow). If sign-in succeeds at the Supabase layer but no local User/Role match is found for that authUserId, this is treated as an error (not silently defaulted to some role) - documented as an expected limitation until Epic 11 (sync) exists, since Users/Roles are currently local-only per device: signing in on a different device than where the account was created/added will correctly fail with "No local role found... sync is not yet implemented."

Sign-out wired into the Settings page placeholder (calls authService.signOut() + clears currentRoleProvider directly for immediate UI feedback; the reactive session stream also independently drives the router back to /sign-in).

Offline nuance (matches the PRD's own flagged assumption/open question, FR-2): reopening the app with an already-cached session works fully offline (currentSession is a local check, no network). A *fresh* sign-in attempt still requires network to call Supabase - this is inherent to any real auth backend and matches the PRD's own [ASSUMPTION]/[NEEDS VALIDATION] framing, not something this implementation needs to solve differently.
close_reason: Sign-in + persistent session implemented (Supabase auth state stream + reactive router gating), 75/75 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 2.1: First-Run Clinic Provisioning & Dentist Sign-Up
description: On a fresh install with no local Clinic data, sign-up creates a Clinic record (locale fr-TN, currency TND), assigns the signing-up User as Dentist with is_clinic_owner=true, and creates Role records for Dentist, Assistant, and Nurse. After sign-up user lands on Today Calendar without re-authentication. Offline during sign-up shows error; no partial data persisted. Unit test confirms all Role records are created. Part of Epic 2 (DocCentral-59s).
notes:
Implemented and tested (11 new tests, 67/67 total passing; 0 analyzer issues).

MAJOR DECISION REVERSAL: switched auth backend from Firebase (kept per an earlier Story 1.1 override) back to Supabase Auth, matching the finalized PRD (FR-1/FR-2) and architecture.md. User confirmed this explicitly after I flagged the conflict. Removed firebase_auth/firebase_core packages and lib/core/firebase/firebase_options.dart entirely; main.dart now calls Supabase.initialize() using String.fromEnvironment('SUPABASE_URL'/'SUPABASE_ANON_KEY') --dart-define values.

IMPORTANT CAVEAT: no real Supabase project is wired anywhere - the user must supply real SUPABASE_URL/SUPABASE_ANON_KEY via --dart-define when actually running the app, or sign-up will fail at the network call (expected, not a bug). Provisioning logic itself is fully unit-tested independent of a real backend via a fake AuthService.

New Drift schema (schemaVersion 2->3): Clinics extended with locale ('fr-TN' default) + currency ('TND' default); new Users/Roles/UserRoles tables (Roles uses @DataClassName('RoleRow') to avoid colliding with the existing Role RBAC enum). ClinicRepositoryImpl.provisionClinic: calls Supabase signUp first (throws AuthException, nothing touches DB on failure/offline), then atomically transacts Clinic + owner User + 3 Role rows (dentist/assistant/nurse, named via Role.name from the existing RBAC enum - no separate string-mapping table) + 1 UserRoles row.

Router: added a redirect gate in app_router.dart based on hasLocalClinicProvider (no clinic -> forced to /sign-up; clinic exists -> can't navigate back to /sign-up). Had to add a refreshListenable (_HasLocalClinicRefreshNotifier) and mark appRouterProvider @Riverpod(keepAlive:true) - without keepAlive the provider (and its internal ref.listen subscription) got disposed after the initial container.read(), breaking redirect evaluation on subsequent navigations. This is a real gotcha worth remembering for any future router-level provider-driven redirect logic.

On successful sign-up, ClinicProvisioningController also calls currentRoleProvider.notifier.setRole(Role.doctor) - this is what makes the RBAC-gated Add patient button (built in Epic 3) actually become visible in a live run for the first time.

Scope boundary respected: session persistence across restarts, sign-in screen, JWT caching are explicitly Story 2.2, not touched here.
close_reason: Sign-up + first-run clinic provisioning implemented against Supabase Auth (reversed from Firebase), 67/67 tests passing, 0 analyzer issues

title: Story 10.3: Reopen Closed Day
description: Dentist reopens a closed day: unlocks counted_cash for re-entry, logs actor/timestamp/reason. Re-entering and confirming updates the record with new counted cash, new delta, new timestamp. Assistant returns PERMISSION_DENIED. Part of Epic 10 (DocCentral-n39).
notes: Implemented: new day_closeout_reopen_logs table + nullable reopenedAt column added to day_closeouts (schema v23). New Permission.canReopenDayCloseout (Doctor-only tier, bumping the hardcoded doctorOnly test list in permission_checker_test.dart from 8 to 9). DayCloseoutRepository.reopenCloseout(role, actorUserId, dayCloseoutId, reason) sets reopenedAt=now on the DayCloseout row and inserts a reopen-log entry (actor/timestamp/reason) in a transaction. confirmCloseout was extended with a branch: if a closeout exists for the date AND its reopenedAt is set (unlocked), it UPDATES that same row in place (new expectedCash recomputed fresh, new countedCash, new delta, new actor/timestamp, clears reopenedAt back to null to re-lock it) instead of throwing DayCloseoutAlreadyExistsException - this is what makes 're-entering and confirming updates the record with new counted cash/delta/timestamp' work without creating a duplicate record. UI: DayCloseoutPage gained a Reopen button (Doctor-only via canReopenDayCloseout, shown only when a locked closeout exists) opening a reason-required dialog (same _UnlockVisitDialog-style pattern used for visit/invoice actions elsewhere); once reopened, the Confirm closeout button reappears (condition changed from closeout==null to closeout==null || closeout.isReopened) letting the same flow re-confirm. Tests: 3 new repository tests (reopen unlocks + logs actor/reason, reconfirm-after-reopen updates same id with new expected/counted/delta and re-locks, PermissionDeniedException for Assistant) + 4 new widget tests (Reopen hidden for Assistant, full reopen-then-confirm-again-shown flow, blank-reason validation error, full reconfirm updates record and re-locks) + new schema v23 test + updated the 2 DayCloseoutRepository fakes for the new interface method. Full suite: 352 tests passing, flutter analyze clean. Committed as 0af8f28 (not pushed, per standing instruction). This was the last story in Epic 10 (Day Closeout, DocCentral-n39) - all 3 stories (10.1-10.3) are now closed.

title: Story 10.2: Record & Confirm Day Closeout
description: Assistant enters counted cash and confirms. Creates Day Closeout record: expected cash (sum of today cash Payments), counted cash (entered), delta (expected - counted), actor, UTC timestamp. One closeout per clinic per date enforced. Non-zero delta flagged visually but does not block confirmation. Unit test confirms delta is always computed, never manually set. Part of Epic 10 (DocCentral-n39).
notes: Implemented: new day_closeouts table (schema v22, no @DataClassName needed since the domain model is DayCloseoutRecord, not colliding with the generated DayCloseout class). description: Originally bundled into Story 4.6 (DocCentral-2mb). Free-text Lab Work Flag note on an appointment, shown in calendar views and the morning side panel. Explicitly dropped from MVP scope during Story 4.1 planning (lab-orders tracking excluded, matching the earlier decision to also drop lab-orders from Patient Management/Epic 3). Deferred rather than closed in case a future scope revision brings lab-order tracking back in.
